prompt:
The depot kiosk software finally moved onto the transit authority's shared CI and their javac runs -Xlint:all -Werror, so our departure-board module is now a build failure. Full output:

    DepotBoard.java:15: warning: [deprecation] Date(int,int,int) in Date has been deprecated
    DepotBoard.java:16: warning: [deprecation] getDay() in Date has been deprecated
    DepotBoard.java:28: warning: [deprecation] Date(int,int,int,int,int) in Date has been deprecated
    DepotBoard.java:29: warning: [deprecation] setMinutes(int) in Date has been deprecated
    DepotBoard.java:30: warning: [deprecation] getHours() in Date has been deprecated
    DepotBoard.java:30: warning: [deprecation] getMinutes() in Date has been deprecated
    DepotBoard.java:73: warning: [removal] runFinalization() in Runtime has been deprecated and marked for removal
    DepotBoard.java:79: warning: [deprecation] getId() in Thread has been deprecated
    error: warnings found and -Werror specified

This module predates java.time and we've been meaning to migrate it properly for years — do that now rather than papering over it: our review policy does not accept @SuppressWarnings, and anything the JDK has actually scheduled for removal should be gone, not annotated. The maths itself is trusted and pinned: service patterns for the June 2026 calendar, the HH:MM rendering including boards that roll past midnight, the exact departure tables, full kiosk boards, and the crew-sheet tags that carry the printing thread's id. Every one of those outputs must come out byte-identical after the migration — TestMain pins them all. Verification is `bash run_verify.sh`; TestMain.java, run_verify.sh and .gitignore are off limits.
notes: 5W4 item 2: deprecation migration to java.time under -Xlint:all -Werror. Authoring toolchain: openjdk/javac 26.0.1. CATALOG PIVOT, documented: Thread.stop/suspend/resume no longer EXIST on JDK 26 (probed 2026-07-15: compile error 'cannot find symbol', removed post-JEP-449), so the 'Thread.stop-adjacent APIs' slot is filled by the surviving thread-adjacent deprecations: Thread.getId() (deprecated, replacement threadId(), identical value for platform threads so behavior-preserving) and [removal]-flagged Runtime.runFinalization() (a cargo-cult 'keep the kiosk lean' call whose correct migration is deletion; prompt's house rule says scheduled-for-removal APIs should be gone). Date internals planted: Date(y,m,d) ctor + getDay() in serviceKind (0=Sun..6=Sat vs DayOfWeek 1=Mon..7=Sun mapping is the classic migration trap), Date(y,m,d,h,min) ctor + setMinutes + getHours/getMinutes in clock() where legacy code abuses Date field normalization as a minute-roller (java.time equivalent: LocalTime.MIDNIGHT.plusMinutes wraps identically, pinned by the 2945-minute case). 7 deprecation + 1 removal warnings; module passes all 19 behavior checks when compiled without the gate, so the fix is purely the migration. Determinism: TestMain pins TimeZone UTC + Locale.ROOT as its first act, and all Date maths happens after main starts; dates chosen (June 2026) are DST-transition-free besides. crewTag expectation computed in-test via threadId() so it is host-independent. Domain check: java-tzslots covers timezone slot conversion as a build task; this is a warnfix migration of wall-clock minute maths with no zones involved — different kind, different layer.
